title: Daniel J. Fisher | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:

755 W Big Beaver Rd Suite 2050
Daniel is a Senior Associate at Fragomen’s Detroit office, where he counsels clients on employment-based nonimmigrant and immigrant visa matters. Daniel has advised individuals, small businesses and multinational corporations across various industries on their immigration matters.
Daniel has experience preparing employment and family-based visa petitions; counseling companies and their in-house counsel on mobility options; drafting legal briefs and memoranda, including complex responses to Requests for Evidence and I-9 auditing; drafting contracts, employment agreements and policies; and conducting research on novel issues pertaining to global mobility. He also has litigation experience.
Prior to his current role, Daniel managed the Orlando, Florida office of a national immigration and personal injury law firm and worked with a Spain-based global relocation company and law firm. He also worked with a UK-based global relocation company, in an outside counsel role, supporting their multinational clients in the automotive, technology, engineering, sports, construction, food and medical industries. Daniel also represented the relocation company at symposiums throughout Europe.
Daniel also has experience liaising with the Department of Labor and U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services. He represented his previous firm and its clients in Washington, DC, where he met with the heads of the Department of Labor, Foreign Labor Division to advocate against changes to the H-2B program and discuss PERM issues.
- Barry University School of Law, J.D., 2004
- University of South Florida, B.A. in International Studies, 2001
- University of South Florida, B.A. in Urban Geography, 2001
Bar Admissions: Florida, Washington, DC
Court Admissions: Fifth Circuit Court of Appeals, U.S. District Court for the Middle District of Florida
Practice Limited to Federal Immigration & Nationality Law
Listed in Martindale-Hubbell Register of Preeminent (AV Rated) Lawyers
Listed in Chambers USA , 2024 to 2025
Named a “Rising Star,” Orlando Style Magazine , 2014
Client Choice Award for Immigration, Avvo , 2011 to 2014

### Publications and lectures
Speaker, “Visados y aspectos migratorios de la movilidad hacia Estados Unidos (Visas and migratory aspects of mobility to the United States).” Jornada de Movilidad Internacional Hacia Estados Unidos: Aspectos Migratorios Y Fiscales (International Mobility Day to the United States: Migratory and Fiscal Aspects) , Madrid, Spain, November 2019.
Speaker, “Short-Term Assignments to the United States.” ABG Personas Association of HR Coordinators , Madrid, Spain, September 2018.
Speaker, “Practical Aspects of Human Resources in the United States.” ABG Personas , Madrid, Spain, September 2018.
Author, “National Interest Waivers for Outstanding Researchers to the U.S.” Ezine , 2014.
Speaker, “Symposium on U.S. Immigration.” University of Central Florida , Orlando, FL, 2012.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
